summary: This source is an abstract for an essay reviewing strategies for creating deformable and stretchable batteries. It highlights the necessity of flexible energy storage for next-generation wearables and identifies key outstanding challenges such as energy density, packaging, and device integration.

principles.statement: Deformable energy storage is essential for next-generation wearable electronics.
principles.rationale: Wearables must interface intimately with human skin, which is hindered by traditional rigid, bulky battery compartments.
principles.source_quote: Deformable energy storage devices are needed to power next-generation wearable electronics that interface intimately with human skin.

signature_quotes.text: Deformable energy storage devices are needed to power next-generation wearable electronics that interface intimately with human skin.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the motivation for developing stretchable batteries.

signature_quotes.text: Currently, deformable energy storage devices demonstrate poor performance compared to their rigid lithium-ion counterparts, forcing wearable manufacturers to design their devices around bulky battery compartments.
signature_quotes.context: Highlighting the current limitations of wearable device design.

anti_patterns.description: Relying on rigid lithium-ion batteries for wearable electronics.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It forces manufacturers to design devices around bulky battery compartments, preventing intimate and comfortable integration with the human body.
